sentence: اِزَّيَّك يَا صَاحْبِي، اِنْتَ كُوَيِّس اِنَّهَارْدَة؟
raw: ازيك يا صاحبي، انت كويس انهاردة؟
ipa: ʔizˈzajjak ˈjaː ˈsˤɑːħbiː ˈʔinta kuˈwajjis ʔinnaˈhaːrda
gloss_en: How are you my friend, are you well today?
notes: greeting izzayyak (badawi-hinds1986 s.v. زي); geminates زّ يّ; Cairene form innaharda

sentence: اِلْجَوّ حَرّ قَوِي فِي اِلصِّيف وِالشَّمْس جَامْدَة.
raw: الجو حر قوي في الصيف والشمس جامدة.
ipa: ilˈɡaww ˈħarr ˈʔawiiː ˈfiː isˤˈsˤiːf wiʃˈʃams ˈɡaːmda
gloss_en: The weather is very hot in summer and the sun is fierce.
notes: ج = /ɡ/ (badawi-hinds1986; watson2002 ch.1: Cairene gīm); sun assimilation aʃ-ʃams with Egyptian reflexes

sentence: مِش عَايِز قَهْوَة، أَنَا بَفَضَّل شَاي بِالنَّعْنَاع.
raw: مش عايز قهوة، أنا بفضل شاي بالنعناع.
ipa: ˈmiʃ ˈʕaːjiz ˈʔahwa ˈʔanaː baˈfɑdˤdˤɑl ˈʃaːj binnaʕˈnaːʕ
gloss_en: I don't want coffee, I prefer tea with mint.
notes: negator miš (badawi-hinds1986 s.v. مش); ق = /ʔ/ in ʔahwa (mitchell1956 §5); bi-imperfect بَفَضَّل

sentence: اِشْتَرِيت تَلَات كِيلُو طَمَاطِم مِن اِلسُّوق بِخَمْسَة جِنِيه.
raw: اشتريت تلات كيلو طماطم من السوق بخمسة جنيه.
ipa: ʔiʃtaˈriːt taˈlaːt ˈkiːluː tˤɑˈmɑːtˤim ˈmin isˈsuːʔ biˈxamsa ɡiˈniːh
gloss_en: I bought three kilos of tomatoes from the market for five pounds.
notes: ث→/t/ in talāta (badawi-hinds1986; watson2002: Cairene stop reflexes); emphatic ط with backing

sentence: هُوَّ فِين اِلْمُدَرِّس بِتَاع اِلْعَرَبِي دِلْوَقْتِي؟
raw: هو فين المدرس بتاع العربي دلوقتي؟
ipa: ˈhuwwa ˈfiːn ilmuˈdarris biˈtaːʕ ilˈʕarabiː dilˈwaʔtiː
gloss_en: Where is the Arabic teacher right now?
notes: genitive exponent bitāʕ (badawi-hinds1986 s.v. بتاع); dilwaʔti with ق=/ʔ/; geminate رّ

sentence: اِسْتَنَّى شْوَيَّة يَا بَاشَا، اِحْنَا ثَلَاثَة.
raw: استنى شوية يا باشا، احنا ثلاثة.
ipa: ʔisˈtannaː ˈʃwajja ˈjaː ˈbaːʃaː ˈʔiħnaː taˈlaːta
gloss_en: Wait a bit, boss — there are three of us.
notes: Cairene imperative istanna and vocative basha (badawi-hinds1986 s.v.); ثلاثة → talata with the stop reflex of the interdental

sentence: اِلْجَوّ مِش حَرّ اِنَّهَارْدَة وِالسَّمَا مْغَيِّمَة.
raw: الجو مش حر انهاردة والسما مغيمة.
ipa: ilˈɡaww ˈmiʃ ˈħarr ʔinnaˈhaːrda ˈwissamaː ˈmɣajjima
gloss_en: The weather is not hot today and the sky is cloudy.
notes: mish negation; innaharda (badawi-hinds1986 s.v.); il- article; geminate yy in mghayyima untouched by EG_MONO (watson2002)

sentence: بِكَام الْكِيلُو؟ بِخَمْسَة جْنَيه وِنُصّ.
raw: بكام الكيلو؟ بخمسة جنيه ونص.
ipa: biˈkaːm alˈkiːluː biˈxamsa ˈɡneːh wiˈnusˤsˤ
gloss_en: How much is the kilo? Five and a half pounds.
notes: bikam question; gneeh with ay→eː (watson2002 ch.2; badawi-hinds1986 s.v. جنيه); wi- conjunction

sentence: اِلْمَعَاد السَّاعَة تَلَاتَة وِنُصّ بَعْد الضُّهْر.
raw: المعاد الساعة تلاتة ونص بعد الضهر.
ipa: ilmaˈʕaːd asˈsaːʕa taˈlaːta wiˈnusˤsˤ ˈbaʕd ɑdˤˈdˤuhr
gloss_en: The appointment is at half past three in the afternoon.
notes: talata stop reflex; emphatic D in ad-duhr; il-/is- articles (badawi-hinds1986)

sentence: أَخُويَا الْكِبِير سَاكِن فِي الْمَدِينَة مَعَ مْرَاتُه وِوْلَادُه.
raw: أخويا الكبير ساكن في المدينة مع مراته وولاده.
ipa: ʔaˈxuːjaː lkiˈbiːr ˈsaːkin ˈfiː lmaˈdiːna ˈmaʕa ˈmraːtuh wiwˈlaːduh
gloss_en: My elder brother lives in the city with his wife and children.
notes: akhuya possessive; mraatuh 'his wife' (badawi-hinds1986 s.v. مرة); initial clusters mr-/wl-

sentence: اِمْشِي عَلَى طُول وِبَعْدَين حَوِّد يِمِين عَنْد الْجَامِع.
raw: امشي على طول وبعدين حود يمين عند الجامع.
ipa: ˈʔimʃiː ˈʕalaː ˈtˤuːl wibaʕˈdeːn ˈħawwid jiˈmiːn ˈʕand alˈɡaːmiʕ
gloss_en: Go straight ahead and then turn right at the mosque.
notes: imperatives imshi/hawwid (badawi-hinds1986 s.v. حود); wi-baʕdeen with ay→eː; gaamiʕ with g

sentence: اَلْأَكْل كَان حِلْو بَسّ الشُّورْبَة كَانِت سَاقْعَة.
raw: الأكل كان حلو بس الشوربة كانت ساقعة.
ipa: alˈʔakl ˈkaːn ˈħilw ˈbass aʃˈʃuːrba ˈkaːnit ˈsaːʔʕa
gloss_en: The food was nice but the soup was cold.
notes: bass 'but'; saqʕa 'cold' with q→ʔ (badawi-hinds1986 s.v. ساقع); kaanit feminine

sentence: فَين حَطَّيت الْمُفْتَاح؟ مِش لَاقِيه.
raw: فين حطيت المفتاح؟ مش لاقيه.
ipa: ˈfeːn ħɑtˤˈtˤeːt almufˈtaːħ ˈmiʃ laːˈʔiːh
gloss_en: Where did you put the key? I can't find it.
notes: feen with ay→eː (watson2002 ch.2); hatteet 2sg perfect; mish + active participle negation

sentence: كَام وَاحِد جَه اِمْبَارِح؟ جُم سَبْعَة.
raw: كام واحد جه امبارح؟ جم سبعة.
ipa: ˈkaːm ˈwaːħid ˈɡah ʔimˈbaːriħ ˈɡum ˈsabʕa
gloss_en: How many came yesterday? Seven came.
notes: kam waahid; geh/gum suppletive 'come' with g (badawi-hinds1986 s.v. جه); imbaarih

sentence: لَو جَيت بَدْرِي نِرُوح مَعَ بَعْض.
raw: لو جيت بدري نروح مع بعض.
ipa: ˈloː ˈɡeːt ˈbadriː niˈruːħ ˈmaʕa ˈbaʕdˤ
gloss_en: If you come early we go together.
notes: law conditional (surface [loː] by the regular EG_MONO_AW rule — law~loː variation, badawi-hinds1986); geet with ay→eː

sentence: رُحْت اِمْبَارِح لِلسُّوق وِجِبْت سَمَك طَازَة.
raw: رحت امبارح للسوق وجبت سمك طازة.
ipa: ˈruħt ʔimˈbaːriħ lisˈsuːʔ wiˈɡibt ˈsamak ˈtˤɑːza
gloss_en: I went to the market yesterday and got fresh fish.
notes: past narrative; is-suuʔ with q→ʔ; wi-gibt; taaza (badawi-hinds1986 s.v. طازة)

sentence: بُكْرَة حَنْسَافِر الصُّبْح بَدْرِي.
raw: بكرة حنسافر الصبح بدري.
ipa: ˈbukra ħanˈsaːfir ɑsˤˈsˤubħ ˈbadriː
gloss_en: Tomorrow we travel early in the morning.
notes: bukra; ha- future prefix (badawi-hinds1986; mitchell1956); emphatic s in is-subh

sentence: أَلُو، مِين مَعَايَا؟ ثَانْيَة وَاحْدَة.
raw: ألو، مين معايا؟ ثانية واحدة.
ipa: ˈʔaluː ˈmiːn maˈʕaːjaː ˈtaːnja ˈwaːħda
gloss_en: Hello, who is this? One second.
notes: miin question; maʕaaya 1sg; tanya with th→t stop reflex (watson2002)

sentence: صَبَاح الْخَير، عَامِل إِيه اِنَّهَارْدَة؟ اَلْحَمْدُ لِلَّه.
raw: صباح الخير، عامل إيه انهاردة؟ الحمد لله.
ipa: sˤɑˈbaːħ alˈxeːr ˈʕaːmil ˈʔiːh ʔinnaˈhaːrda lˈħamdu ˈlillah
gloss_en: Good morning, how are you doing today? Praise God.
notes: ʕaamil eeh greeting frame (badawi-hinds1986 s.v. ايه); il-kheer with ay→eː; formulaic il-hamdu lillah

sentence: اَلصَّبْر طَيِّب يَا صَاحْبِي.
raw: الصبر طيب يا صاحبي.
ipa: ɑsˤˈsˤɑbr ˈtˤajjib ˈjaː ˈsˤɑːħbiː
gloss_en: Patience is good, my friend.
notes: proverb-like closing; emphatic backing in is-sabr; sahbi vocative (badawi-hinds1986 s.v. صاحب)
